Great salespeople are inspired by failure. I know that's whacked right? There's a hate failure who doesn't but they somehow twisted this thing. They're encouraged by it. They're fueled by it. Recently I lost a client okay and I remember when I hung up the phone I'm like dude this guy's got me into serious serious inspiration here because I hate failing. I hate it and I know it's not good for me and so what do I do? I go back to preparing. I go back to being interested. I go back to pushing that necessity level where I'm like I refuse to lose another client like this. I refuse to make that mistake again. Look everyone hates failing. You should hate it too but don't just hate it. Learn to use it. That's what the greats do. They don't like it. They don't they don't want to fail. They'd love to win with everybody but they now use that as wood on a fire to blow this fire of greatness up so they're inspired not to let it happen again. Also with that sold customer that I lost it might be a failure. It serves as inspiration. Don't think I'm taking those people off my list okay. I'm gonna go back and figure out how to put them together and build them back into my clientele and put them back into my business.
